behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the target file codegen-project-annotations.ts, references the djb2 algorithm via the cse.yorku.ca URL, and gives reference hash values (5381, 177670, 193485963) for empty, 'a', and 'abc'.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes properties (position-sensitivity, no collisions, reference values) and cites external algorithm reference, but doesn't show the exact djb2 code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_hash_function_exists only checks 'function hash' exists, but test_typescript_compiles and other tests would pass with any collision-free position-sensitive hash (FNV, etc.); however the hidden test file (truncated) may check reference values 5381/177670/193485963 which would force djb2 specifically—though instruction also specifies those values, so it's consistent. Tests shown accept any collision-free hash, which is fine.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests extract the hash function body and execute it via Node.js with new Function(), checking behavioral properties.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual position-sensitive, collision-free hash behavior across diverse strings; a stub returning a constant would fail ALL_UNIQUE check.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover position-sensitivity, anagram distinction, pnpm-path collisions, and compilation — matching the instruction's stated requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are separately named with docstrings explaining each property being verified.

no_typos.reason: Paths and filenames appear consistent across instruction, tests, and solution.
